name: Mokuleia
desc: End-of-the-road ranch country — farms, Dillingham Airfield, polo grounds, gloriously empty beaches.
insider: Dillingham Airfield does tandem skydiving and glider rides — the North Shore aerial views are absolutely unreal
Mokuleia Beach is almost always empty — but rip currents are strong and dangerous, so swim with extreme caution
Hawaii Polo Club matches on Sunday afternoons are a unique hidden experience — bring a picnic and sit on the grass
Kaena Point trail starts at the end of Farrington Highway — the Laysan albatross nesting colony at the tip is extraordinary
The road literally ends here — beyond Dillingham there is nothing but ranch land, beach, and wilderness
Camp Erdman (YMCA) at the end of the road hosts retreats with a beautiful, tranquil oceanfront setting
Parts of Lost were filmed at the Mokuleia airstrip — the crashed plane fuselage prop was here for years
Beach sunsets are the most dramatic on Oahu — absolutely nothing on the western ocean horizon to block the view
Strong trade winds make Mokuleia one of the best kite surfing and windsurfing spots on the island
No stores, no gas stations, no restaurants past Waialua — fill up your tank and pack food before heading out
